Off Calendar Jul-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBIN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AGAINST DEFENDANTS CHRISTIE BARRETT WEST, TIMOTHY MCCALL WEST, THOMAS WILLIAM WEST

Appearances: Mark Hooshmand of the Hooshmand Law Group (415.423.3400) for plaintiffs and moving parties; Frank Kim of National Legal Associates (415.567.5700) for defendants and responding parties.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED, subject to the modifications below. Plaintiff is entitled to attorney fees because this lawsuit was a reasonable and necessary cost of enforcing the judgment in the 2018 case. The Court reduces Mr. Hooshmand's hourly rate to $720 an hour and Mr. Swanson's rate to $200 an hour. The Court also adopts a 1.3x multiplier, given Plaintiff's counsel's skill in navigating the case, the novelty and difficulty of the issues presented, and the preclusive effect the case has had on counsel's work on other cases. In total, Plaintiff is entitled to $256,087 in attorney fees. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Code Compliant Discovery Responses From Defendant Christie West To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Christie Barrett West
Off Calendar Feb-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, AND NINA ROBIN's Motion To Compel Code Compliant Discovery Responses From Defendant Christie West To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Christie Barrett West.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion of plaintiffs Jennifer Sarkany et al. (Plaintiffs) for an order compelling defendant Christie West to provide further responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Form Interrogatories, Set One, and for sanctions, is granted in part and denied in part.

The requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) As to Form Interrogatory 15.1, Plaintiff has shown that defendant's response is incomplete or evasive. Defendant Christie West is ordered to provide further verified responses within 20 days of notice of entry of this order.

With respect to the Special Interrogatories, however, defendant Christie West has provided verified answers giving straightforward and nonevasive responses that she does not have the information Plaintiffs request. Plaintiffs believe that she should have the information, or doubt the veracity of her statement denying information, but the verified interrogatory responses are sufficient and a motion to compel does not provide a vehicle for the court to test the truthfulness or plausibility of the responses. The motion to compel further responses is denied as to these interrogatories.

In light of the mixed results Plaintiffs achieved with this motion, the court finds that circumstances would make imposition of sanctions unjust and does not award sanctions on this motion.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
